opp.name: Khansaheb
opp.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP/Champion (Abraham, - Bilal the EB was unavailable) - he confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (new construction projects are paused, though they have several years of projects underway) - their current concern is the materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade & fuel prices - I have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins & unlock better materials pricing), offered to present to upper mgt and/or bring Carl in in helpful to show how we can help strengthen their economics, he will talk to Bilal. 16th April - Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one, (wouldn't say who else) - current expectation is that they will resume in Q2 - moving back to end Q2. 25th Mar - Response that the project is paused due to the war priorities: " Given the present conditions, we have a delay in the closure of this project. We will provide further updates should there be any changes." appears we are still in the mix as one of their options. 13th Mar - Reached out again for an update (several out of office responses) and requested a meeting after the Eid break. 17th Feb - Same response, not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant . The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments". 16th Feb - Gone quiet since they refocussed on a reduced scope, escalated to senior EB requesting a f2f this week, reducing confidence to 5%. 16th Jan - Pushing to get a meeting while Tanya is in town end Jan/early Feb, Abraham will get back to us. 20th Dec - Pushed a couple of times for updates - response 'management are evaluating, will update in the new year' 19th Nov - As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our 'min prepared to support', still a small deal but could be acceptable, let's see their response, if negative its dead. Dropped confidence to 5%. 30th Oct - Commercial discussion held, changes in approach, now looking to run a pilot approach to a business unit before deploying to wider business , reduces the scope and also changed sourcing to a later phase - now on the cusp of being too small, will get clarity once there are eInvoicing estimates of usage - moved close date back to Q4 and confidence down to 10% 28th Oct - Call scheduled Thurs 30th to discuss commercial estimation. 22nd Oct - Nudged to see if they need help with completing the commercial estimation together. 14th Oct - POC went well, they are asking for pricing, have shared the estimation template and offer to run through it together. 13th Oct - POC with Bilal (Exec Sponsor) confirmed onsite scheduled for 14th Oct (with Tanya & Dan onsite) 7th Oct - POC held onsite with Khansaheb senior manager (Abraham) and Crowe consultants, IF successful they will escalate to another session with Bilal (Exec sponsor) 23rd Sept - Pre-POC meeting set for 29th to review POC prior to live demo session (later that week TBC). 11th Sept - POC details shared to run a live demo .... pilots team tied up for next few weeks Tanya will have to build delaying the process. 25th Aug - No response from Ravi, called and postponed (also not clear Abraham is back yet) - some option offered, no response yet. 14th Aug - Ravi scheduled 26th but we have a clash, requested to reschedule. 7th Aug - Demo'ed to Bilal (GM) and exec sponsor, went well, very engaged - next step to build a POC demo based on their processes when Abrahim returns on 26th (maybe run it on 10th when Tanya/Carl are in Dubai? TBC). 31st July - Ravi called to say that the GM (Bilal) doesn't want to wait for Abraham's response - demo being arranged for next Weds-Fri. 29th July - Presented to Abraham (HOP), went well, feedback was positive, next steps are: follow up demo on invoicing & Sourcing, to sign an NDA, to get a proposal and present to management prior to a decision, shared the pricing estimate template and NDA underway. - moved to Formal Evaluation. 22nd July - Following demo request had a disco call with their consultant (Ravi - Crowe UAE) managing the project, interested in S2C but priority focus is supplier management- next step is to present to head of procurement, will be presenting iSupplier/ZSN, iRisk and iSource- scheduled for 29th Jul20

metrics.narrative: Khansaheb's stated MEDDPICC metrics are fast supplier onboarding, improved compliance, reduced manual processing, and automated 3-way invoice matching (SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c, updated 19 Feb 2026), which line up with the operational pain Abraham Mathew and the Crowe consultants walked through in the 14 Oct 2025 and 30 Oct 2025 Avoma sessions (manual KYC/AML checks, expired-document tracking, three-way match against GRN and PO). No dollar figure or headcount saving was ever quantified for Khansaheb specifically; the closest to a number is the new pain Abraham raised on 18 May 2026, that materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade and fuel prices (Next_Step_History__c), which the rep is now trying to tie to a procuretech margin-protection business case. Quantifying that margin impact with Abraham is the next move to firm up this element.

champion.narrative: Abraham Mathew, Khansaheb's Head of Procurement, has been the one constant across every call and Next Step update since the 22 Jul 2025 discovery call through 18 May 2026, running POCs, sharing sample documents, and pushing internally for meetings (SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c names him explicitly as 'actively involved in POCs, providing detailed feedback, willing to share sample documents', updated 19 Feb 2026). He continues to advocate on 18 May 2026 by offering to relay the rep's margin-protection pitch to Bilal Hamadeh and floating an upper-management presentation. What has NOT developed in nearly a year is EB conversion: Abraham has never yet delivered a direct Zycus-Bilal conversation or a funded budget decision, so he is best read as a developing, at-risk champion rather than a proven one.

competition.narrative: Khansaheb confirmed on 16 Apr 2026 that it had shortlisted two vendors with Zycus as one of them ('wouldn't say who else', Next_Step_History__c), and reaffirmed the same two-vendor shortlist on 18 May 2026. The rival's identity has never been disclosed in any call or field, so it is tracked under Salesforce's Competitors__c value of 'Others.' SAP S/4HANA appears throughout the 2025 calls only as Khansaheb's existing ERP and integration target (CSV export, later SFTP), never as a competing bid. With no fresh signal since 18 May 2026 (spend still frozen, ~7 weeks quiet), the wedge to press once budget resumes is the materials-cost/margin-protection narrative Abraham raised, which an unnamed generalist rival is unlikely to be pitching.

sources: Avoma call notes, 14 Oct 2025 and 30 Oct 2025
identify_pain.narrative: Abraham Mathew and the Crowe consultants walked through concrete operational pain across two 2025 sessions: manual, spreadsheet-driven vendor onboarding with no validation on document expiry ('Suppliers are unable to submit their registration forms due to incomplete mandatory fields', Avoma notes 14 Oct 2025), and manual invoice handling with government-portal integration failures (30 Oct 2025). On 18 May 2026 Abraham raised a newer, externally-driven pain: material prices have risen sharply because of the blockade and fuel costs, which the rep is now using to reposition Zycus as margin protection rather than just process automation. Abraham is the clear pain owner throughout.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or formal contract discussion has taken place; the furthest the deal has gone commercially is a 3-year-minimum SaaS term and phased pricing scenarios discussed on 30 Oct 2025 ('the minimum contract term is three years due to front-loaded SaaS costs', Avoma call). eInvoicing paper is additionally gated on the UAE FDA e-invoicing portal: Khansaheb flagged that vendors must go through a nominated intermediary and that government-API integration failures must be minimized (30 Oct 2025 call), and Zycus had not confirmed which FDA-approved intermediary it uses. Getting a written proposal in front of Bilal once spend unfreezes is what would create paper-process visibility.

sources: Avoma call, 7 Aug 2025 and 13-14 Oct 2025 onsite
economic_buyer.narrative: Bilal Hamadeh is named as GM in both the CRM MEDDPICC record ('Bilal (taking project forward)', updated 19 Feb 2026) and the Next Step trail, where he was demoed to on 7 Aug 2025 and joined the 13-14 Oct 2025 onsite POC as exec sponsor. Since that onsite, however, Bilal has had no direct Zycus conversation: he was unavailable for the most recent 18 May 2026 meeting, where Abraham Mathew relayed only that 'he will talk to Bilal' rather than Bilal engaging directly. This is a real absence anomaly for a deal this far along (over 7 months without EB contact as of this sweep), and re-securing a direct Bilal conversation, ideally with a Zycus executive present, is the single highest-leverage next move.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself maps the process: Ravi Shankar (the Crowe UAE consultant running the project) leads every session, Abraham Mathew supplies procurement detail, and Bilal Hamadeh is looped in only at gate moments (the 7 Aug 2025 demo and the 13-14 Oct 2025 onsite POC) rather than every call, consistent with Ravi Shankar's own framing that the pilot 'will go to management (via Bilal) for review and approval; no false commitments at this stage' (Avoma call, 30 Oct 2025). As of 18 May 2026 the process is stalled at that same gate: Abraham confirmed the pilot is still shortlisted but that Bilal has not yet reviewed it with management because non-essential spend is frozen, with no dated milestone for when review resumes.

decision_criteria.narrative: The 14 Oct 2025 onsite (Zycus Khansaheb - Next Steps) confirmed the evaluation criteria in detail: self-service vendor onboarding with configurable KYC/AML document checks and expiry alerts, an automated prequalification scoring engine, three-way invoice matching via the relevant stakeholder, and ERP integration (initially CSV, later SFTP two-way). These match the CRM's decision-criteria picklist (vendor portal with self-registration/KYC-AML, 3-way invoice matching, ERP integration, automation, compliance, cost efficiency; SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c updated 19 Feb 2026). Merlin intake/insights was also demoed and drew a direct buyer question ('is that an add-on, or standard?', 14 Oct 2025) though it was explicitly paused from active discussion on the 30 Oct 2025 commercial call as scope narrowed.

items.summary: Mohamad Alhakim (Zycus RVP Middle East) emailed Abraham to introduce himself as the new primary contact after Dan's departure, signaling he's reviewed the deal history and is ready to pick up discussions; he's offering to meet in person or virtually but the next move is on Abraham to signal timing.

day_summary.overall: After a period of stalled contact following the previous rep Dan's departure, Zycus re-engaged Khansaheb with a new point of contact taking over the account. The new RVP introduced himself directly to Abraham, acknowledged the external challenges that had paused the deal, and put the ball in Khansaheb's court to resume talks when ready — so the deal now sits waiting on Abraham to respond.

north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-08-31, today 2026-07-08, 54 days to close. Stage Shortlisted, dwell since roughly late 2025 through repeated re-scoping (Q4 2025 -> Q1 2026 -> end-Q2 2026 -> 31 Aug 2026 close). Rep confidence has swung 10% (Oct 2025) -> 5% (Nov 2025/Feb 2026) -> 50% (18 May 2026) on verbal reassurance only, with no dated milestone locked and no EB contact in over 7 months.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is still one of two shortlisted providers, but the deal is stalled: economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh has been unreachable since the Oct 2025 onsite and a regional conflict/blockade has frozen Khansaheb's non-essential spend with no lift date, against a close date only 54 days away.

flags: Single-threaded on Abraham since Bilal (the real EB/GM) has been unreachable since ~18 May 2026 - open a second Khansaheb thread (Bilal directly, or another management contact) before the pause lifts so Zycus isn't dependent on one intermediary.

Relationship-continuity risk: Dan Quinn (11 months of built history with Abraham) departed and Mohamad Alhakim took over 29 Jun 2026 with no reply yet to his 3 Jul introduction - a warm, context-rich handoff call is needed to preserve the relationship equity built over the past year.

The rival vendor named in the 2-vendor shortlist has never been identified and no competitive refresh has run since 18 May 2026 (>30 days) - ask Abraham directly who it is and reinforce the Merlin/single-platform wedge against Khansaheb's fragmented 4-ERP environment.
Reconfirm the compelling event: get a written estimate of when the spend freeze lifts so the 31 Aug 2026 close date and Best Case forecast can be recalibrated honestly.
The competitive shortlist has only ever named 'one other provider' without identifying it; use Abraham to learn who it is and where they are weaker before pricing conversations resume.

The economic buyer (Bilal Hamadeh) has not engaged Zycus directly since the Oct 2025 POC; converting Abraham's 18 May 2026 offer to broker a Bilal conversation into an actual meeting is the highest-leverage move to unstick the deal.

The pilot has been scoped down to a single business unit that Khansaheb itself called 'on the cusp of being too small'; reinforcing the group-wide phase-two story discussed on the 30 Oct 2025 commercial call would help justify the deal's size to the review committee.
Convert Abraham's own offer to present to upper management into a scheduled executive session with Carl Kimball before the blockade-driven freeze causes the pilot to be shelved indefinitely.
Reconfirm competitive standing against the unnamed second shortlisted vendor once evaluation resumes, since Khansaheb has given no read on the two finalists since the process paused in late 2025.

Champion Abraham Mathew has never delivered direct EB access to Bilal Hamadeh in nearly a year; an executive-to-executive connect via the deal owner's manager is now the single highest-leverage lever to unstick this deal.

The pilot scope has shrunk to iSupplier plus eInvoicing only, which both sides flagged as sub-scale (30 Oct 2025); anchoring the business case to the blockade-driven materials-cost pain Abraham raised on 18 May 2026 is the strongest current path to re-justify the investment.

With only one named rival ('Others') and no visibility into their standing, using the next Abraham contact to press for even directional competitive detail would sharpen the win read before spend unfreezes.

win_position.text: Khansaheb's Head of Procurement Abraham Mathew told us directly on 18 May 2026 that Zycus is still one of two shortlisted providers, keeping us alive despite a months-long spend freeze.
Economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh has not held a direct Zycus conversation since the Oct 2025 onsite POC and was unavailable again on 18 May 2026, leaving Abraham as our only line into the account.
The pilot has been cut down to a single-BU iSupplier plus eInvoicing deployment that both Khansaheb and Zycus flagged as bordering too small to be worth pursuing on the 30 Oct 2025 commercial call.

deal_momentum.text: Rep confidence on this deal swung from 10% (Oct 2025) to 5% (Nov 2025/Feb 2026) back up to 50% (18 May 2026) purely on Abraham's verbal reassurance, with no hard milestone locked in.
After months of one-line 'still under review' replies, Abraham gave a substantive update on 18 May 2026, reconfirming the shortlist and raising a fresh business angle around materials-cost inflation.

customer_commitment.text: Abraham has stayed engaged through every POC, commercial call, and status check for nearly a year and offered on 18 May 2026 to raise Zycus with Bilal Hamadeh directly.
No Khansaheb contact beyond Abraham has proactively reached out to Zycus since the Oct 2025 onsite; the buyer email received 19 May 2026 was a reply, not a fresh initiation.

We will provide further updates should there be any changes."<span style="font-family: Tahoma,sans-serif; font-size: 11pt;"> appears we are still in the mix as one of their options. 13th Mar - Reached out again for an update (several out of office responses) and requested a meeting after the Eid break. 17th Feb - Same response, not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant. The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments". 16th Feb - Gone quiet since they refocussed on a reduced scope, escalated to senior EB requesting a f2f this week, reducing confidence to 5%. 16th Jan - Pushing to get a meeting while Tanya is in town end Jan/early Feb, Abraham will get back to us. 20th Dec - Pushed a couple of times for updates - response 'management are evaluating, will update in the new year' 19th Nov - As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our 'min prepared to support', still a small deal but could be acceptable, let's see their response, if negative its dead. Dropped confidence to 5%. 30th Oct - Commercial discussion held, changes in approach, now looking to run a pilot approach to a business unit before deploying to wider business, reduces the scope and also changed sourcing to a later phase - now on the cusp of being too small, will get clarity once there are eInvoicing estimates of usage - moved close date back to Q4 and confidence down to 10% 28th Oct - Call scheduled Thurs 30th to discuss commercial estimation. 22nd Oct - Nudged to see if they need help with completing the commercial estimation together. 14th Oct - POC went well, they are asking for pricing, have shared the estimation template and offer to run through it together. 13th Oct - POC with Bilal (Exec Sponsor) confirmed onsite scheduled for 14th Oct (with Tanya & Dan onsite) 7th Oct - POC held onsite with Khansaheb senior manager (Abraham) and Crowe consultants, IF successful they will escalate to another session with Bilal (Exec sponsor) 23rd Sept - Pre-POC meeting set for 29th to review POC prior to live demo session (later that week TBC). 11th Sept - POC details shared to run a live demo.... pilots team tied up for next few weeks Tanya will have to build delaying the process. 25th Aug - No response from Ravi, called and postponed (also not clear Abraham is back yet) - some option offered, no response yet. 14th Aug - Ravi scheduled 26th but we have a clash, requested to reschedule. 7th Aug - Demo'ed to Bilal (GM) and exec sponsor, went well, very engaged - next step to build a POC demo based on their processes when Abrahim returns on 26th (maybe run it on 10th when Tanya/Carl are in Dubai? TBC). 31st July - Ravi called to say that the GM (Bilal) doesn't want to wait for Abraham's response - demo being arranged for next Weds-Fri. 29th July - Presented to Abraham (HOP), went well, feedback was positive, next steps are: follow up demo on invoicing & Sourcing, to sign an NDA, to get a proposal and present to management prior to a decision, shared the pricing estimate template and NDA underway. - moved to Formal Evaluation. 22nd July - Following demo request had a disco call with their consultant (Ravi - Crowe UAE) managing the project, interested in S2C but priority focus is supplier management- next step is to present to head of procurement, will be presenting iSupplier/ZSN, iRisk and iSource- scheduled for 29th Jul20
